title: Market Brief — Saturday, June 13, 2026
summary: GPU markets are loose and loosening fast. Util-avail stands at 37% for A100, 38% for H100, 61% for H200, and 54% for B200, with H200 in outright oversupply territory. Utilization collapsed across the board over 30 days, with H200 plunging 57.6 percentage points, the steepest drop. A100 spot fell 7.3% day-over-day to $0.97/hr, the biggest single-day price move in the stack.
paragraphs: GPU markets are loose and loosening fast. Util-avail stands at 37% for A100, 38% for H100, 61% for H200, and 54% for B200, with H200 in outright oversupply territory. Utilization collapsed across the board over 30 days, with H200 plunging 57.6 percentage points, the steepest drop. A100 spot fell 7.3% day-over-day to $0.97/hr, the biggest single-day price move in the stack.

At TCO-adjusted cost (1.25x spot), running Llama 70B on H100 costs roughly $8.88/M tokens. DeepSeek V3.2 at $0.34/M output and Meta Llama 3.3 70B at $0.32/M are selling far below that floor, burning cash at losses exceeding $8/M tokens. Grades: OpenAI Strong at premium tiers, Anthropic Neutral, Google Neutral, DeepSeek Burning Cash, Meta Burning Cash.

B200 is undervalued: at $4.38/hr spot and $3.20/M token cost, it is the only GPU class delivering positive margins at the high tier (47%), making it the clear infrastructure buy. H100 is overvalued relative to its collapsing utilization and negative margins through the high tier; short H100 capacity, go long B200 allocation.
bullets: 61% util-avail on H200 signals oversupply, the loosest GPU class in the stack.
62.2% H100 utilization today is down 37.1 percentage points in 30 days.
$0.18/M output from OpenAI's gpt-oss-120b is the cheapest frontier-adjacent token price ever recorded.
212.9% annualized volatility on H200 OCPI makes it the most unstable compute contract trading.
$8.54/M token loss is what DeepSeek V3.2 burns per million output tokens against H100 TCO.
21.78 on the LLMflation Index means frontier token costs have fallen 78% since GPT-4 launch.
